display.purpose: Hero headlines and major story openings only.

body.purpose: Navigation, controls, metadata, practical guidance, and curator workflows.

arabic-display.purpose: Arabic place, story, and hero titles with generous line-height.

shadows.name: heritage-ambient
shadows.purpose: Resting documentary cards where tonal separation alone is insufficient.

shadows.name: document-lift
shadows.purpose: Interactive card lift and selected media surfaces.

shadows.name: elevated-overlay
shadows.purpose: Dialogs, menus, and map popovers only.

motion.name: ease-heritage
motion.purpose: Ease-out for arrival, selection, and spatial transitions.

motion.name: ease-in-out-heritage
motion.purpose: Shared movement between map/list and gallery states.

motion.name: route-duration
motion.purpose: Fast route and control transitions that do not delay field work.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Disable smooth scrolling, shimmer, parallax, and transform-heavy reveals.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Decisive action for exploring, saving, or continuing a route.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--color-terracotta-600); color: var(--color-stone-50); padding: 0.625rem 1.25rem; min-height: 2.75rem; border: 0; border-radius: 0.5rem; font: 500 0.875rem/1.4 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.23,1,0.32,1), transform 120ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--color-terracotta-700); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(0); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-terracotta-500); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: var(--color-teal-600); color: var(--color-stone-50); padding: 0.625rem 1.25rem; min-height: 2.75rem; border: 0; border-radius: 0.5rem; font: 500 0.875rem/1.4 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.23,1,0.32,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--color-teal-500); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-terracotta-500); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Search Field
components.description: Visible-label search field for monument and story discovery.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: 0.375rem; color: var(--color-stone-900); font: 500 0.875rem/1.4 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-field input { min-height: 2.75rem; width: 100%; padding: 0.625rem 0.75rem; background: var(--color-card); color: var(--color-stone-900); border: 1px solid var(--color-parchment-300); border-radius: 0.5rem; font: 400 1rem/1.5 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-field input::placeholder { color: var(--color-stone-700); } .ds-field input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-terracotta-500); outline-offset: 2px; border-color: var(--color-terracotta-600); }

components.name: Documentary Card
components.description: Image-led place or story card using verified media and source context.
components.html:
Mamluk period

Monument name

Short historical or practical context.
components.css: .ds-card { overflow: hidden; background: var(--color-card); color: var(--color-stone-900); border: 1px solid var(--color-parchment-300); border-radius: 0.5rem; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px oklch(14% 0.025 45 / 0.12), 0 1px 3px oklch(14% 0.025 45 / 0.08); transition: box-shadow 180ms cubic-bezier(0.23,1,0.32,1), transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.23,1,0.32,1); } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 4px 16px oklch(14% 0.025 45 / 0.10), 0 1px 4px oklch(14% 0.025 45 / 0.06); transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-card-media { min-height: 10rem; background: var(--color-stone-300); } .ds-card-body { padding: 1.25rem; } .ds-card-kicker { margin: 0 0 0.375rem; color: var(--color-teal-600); font: 500 0.75rem/1.4 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card h3 { margin: 0; font: 600 1.25rem/1.35 'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif; } .ds-card p { margin: 0.625rem 0 0; font: 400 1rem/1.6 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; }

components.name: Period Chip
components.description: Period or status marker that never carries meaning by color alone.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; min-height: 1.75rem; padding: 0.25rem 0.625rem; border-radius: 9999px; background: oklch(55% 0.16 42 / 0.15); color: oklch(40% 0.16 42); border: 1px solid oklch(55% 0.16 42 / 0.3); font: 500 0.75rem/1.4 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; }

components.name: Grouped Navigation
components.description: Task-grouped public navigation for Explore, Learn, Personal, and Studio.
components.html: ExploreLearnPersonal
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.25rem; } .ds-nav-link { padding: 0.625rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; color: var(--color-stone-700); text-decoration: none; font: 500 0.875rem/1.4 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; transition: background 160ms ease-out, color 160ms ease-out; } .ds-nav-link:hover { background: var(--color-parchment-100); color: var(--color-stone-900); } .ds-nav-link-active { background: oklch(48% 0.17 42 / 0.10); color: var(--color-terracotta-700); } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-terracotta-500); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Cinematic Page Intro
components.description: Dark documentary arrival treatment with one clear next action.
components.html:
Cairo, through time

Walk through civilization

Discover monuments, stories, and routes across the historic city.
components.css: .ds-intro { min-height: 24rem; display: grid; align-items: end; padding: 3rem 1.5rem; color: var(--color-stone-50); background: var(--color-stone-950); } .ds-intro-content { max-width: 42rem; } .ds-intro-kicker { margin: 0 0 0.75rem; color: var(--color-gold-400); font: 500 0.875rem/1.4 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-intro h1 { margin: 0; color: var(--color-stone-50); font: 600 clamp(2.5rem, 7vw, 5rem)/1.1 'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif; letter-spacing: -0.025em; text-wrap: balance; } .ds-intro p:not(.ds-intro-kicker) { max-width: 65ch; margin: 1rem 0 1.5rem; color: var(--color-stone-200); font: 400 1rem/1.6 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; }

narrative.overview:
Minarets of Cairo is a product UI with the atmosphere of a documentary field guide. The interface should feel like an archive that moves with the traveler: rooted in evidence, alive with place, and easy to use while walking through a historic neighborhood. Verified photography, map context, period color, captions, and bilingual typography carry more identity than ornament.

The system is curious, reverent, and vivid. It uses dark stone surfaces for arrival and cinematic focus, parchment and white surfaces for reading and planning, terracotta for decisive action, teal for orientation, and gold for rare moments of discovery. Brightness comes from contrast, real imagery, and clear wayfinding rather than saturated decoration. Motion may create a sense of arrival and continuity, but content remains visible without it and every transition has a reduced-motion alternative.
keyCharacteristics: Bilingual English/Arabic parity with generous Arabic rhythm.
Real place imagery and visible evidence.
Controlled cinematic motion that serves navigation and state.
Bright discovery moments inside a restrained product vocabulary.

rules.name: The Evidence Color Rule
rules.body: Accent color marks action, selection, status, or place context. It never exists only to make a card look lively.

rules.name: The Brightness-Through-Place Rule
rules.body: When a screen needs more brightness, add verified photography, a clear period accent, or a stronger surface contrast before adding more saturated decoration.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Use serif or Arabic editorial type for place and story. Use Source Sans 3 for product actions, navigation, status, and data.

rules.name: The Reading-Room Rule
rules.body: Arabic text receives generous line-height and sufficient measure. Never compress Arabic to match Latin block height.

rules.name: The Quiet Depth Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are calm at rest. Elevation appears only when a layer floats, receives focus, or changes state.

rules.name: The No-Ghost-Card Rule
rules.body: Never pair a 1px decorative border with a shadow of 16px or more on the same card or button.

dos: Do let verified photography, maps, captions, context, and source notes carry the identity.
Do preserve English/Arabic parity in content, actions, hierarchy, and states.
Do use terracotta for primary actions, teal for supporting orientation, and gold for rare discovery emphasis.
Do keep body text contrast at or above 4.5:1 and verify overlay text against every image treatment.
Do make loading, empty, error, success, disabled, and authentication states explicit.
Do use cinematic entrance motion only when it communicates arrival or continuity; keep content visible if motion fails.
Do provide reduced-motion behavior that disables smooth scrolling, shimmer, parallax, and transform-heavy reveals.
Do use semantic HTML before ARIA and provide list alternatives for maps.
Do use real verified media before decorative placeholders.
Do design for both research at home and fast lookup while walking in bright outdoor conditions.
donts: Don't use generic SaaS dashboards or productivity-tool visual language for public heritage experiences.
Don't flatten monuments into sterile databases or tourist checklists.
Don't use decorative ancient-Egypt or generic heritage clichés without historical purpose.
Don't use overloaded card grids, fake imagery, or AI-generated visual filler.
Don't create separate English and Arabic experiences with unequal meaning or functionality.
Don't use repeating stripe backgrounds, gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, or full-page motion choreography.
Don't use colored side-stripe borders on notices, cards, or list items.
Don't combine a 1px decorative border with a 16px-plus shadow on cards or buttons.
Don't over-round cards, sections, inputs, or panels. Content cards stop at 12px radius.
Don't use display fonts for controls, labels, navigation, or dense curator UI.
Don't use color, hover, pointer position, or animation as the only way to understand or operate a feature.
Don't expose dead actions such as fake Offline downloads or non-functional Compare cards.
